prompt:
You are Agent 9 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-zStylz.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook).

Specifically:
1. Check coordination context — the previous session (Session 8) added SMS support and flagged that user preferences need updating.
2. Review the current user preferences model and service from Session 3.
3. The original preferences assumed email-only. This assumption is no longer valid — SMS was added in Session 8.
4. Refactor the preferences model to support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook).
5. Update the preferences service to handle multi-channel preferences.
6. Update the notification service to check per-channel preferences before sending.
7. Update tests to cover multi-channel scenarios.
8. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- This is a conscious decision change — the email-only assumption from Session 3 is explicitly invalidated.
- Preserve backward compatibility where possible (existing email preferences should still work).
- Document the decision change and why.

parameters.task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook). Refactor preferences model from email-only to per-channel settings.

parameters.new_context: SMS and webhook channels now exist. The email-only assumption is invalid. Preferences model has been refactored to support per-channel settings (email, sms, webhook) with independent enabled/categories per channel. NotificationService now checks preferences for all channels, not just email.

parameters.summary: Refactored user preferences from email-only to per-channel (email, SMS, webhook) with shared ChannelPreferences base interface
parameters.context: SMS adapter was added in Session 8 and webhook existed from earlier. The original preferences model (decision 01KN8EJPXVSQ9ZCWQ6B1SPSTY9) assumed email-only. Notifications sent via SMS/webhook bypassed preference checks entirely.
parameters.rationale: Each channel gets independent enabled/categories settings via ChannelPreferences base interface. EmailPreferences extends it with frequency. UserPreferences has explicit email/sms/webhook properties. PreferencesService gained a generic shouldSend(userId, channel, type) method. NotificationService checks preferences for all channels now, not just email. Backward compatibility preserved: shouldSendEmail() still works, existing email-only UpdatePreferencesInput still valid.

alternatives.option: Use Record<NotificationChannel, ChannelPreferences> instead of explicit properties
alternatives.reason_rejected: Loses type safety for email-specific frequency field. Explicit properties allow EmailPreferences to extend ChannelPreferences with extra fields while keeping sms/webhook simple.

alternatives.option: Keep email-only preferences and add separate shouldSendSms/shouldSendWebhook methods
alternatives.reason_rejected: Does not scale — each new channel requires a new method. Generic shouldSend(userId, channel, type) with channelToPreferencesKey mapping is cleaner.

assumptions: Three channels (email, sms, webhook) are sufficient for now
All channels default to enabled with all categories
in_app channel has no preferences mapping yet — shouldSend returns true for unmapped channels

parameters.summary: Agent 9: Refactored preferences to multi-channel (email/SMS/webhook). All 231 tests pass. Supersedes email-only decision.
parameters.detail:
Changes made:
1. src/models/user-preferences.ts — Added ChannelPreferences base interface, SmsPreferences, WebhookPreferences. UserPreferences now has email/sms/webhook properties. Added channelToPreferencesKey() helper, DEFAULT_SMS_CATEGORIES, DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_CATEGORIES.
2. src/services/preferences.service.ts — Added generic shouldSend(userId, channel, type). shouldSendEmail() now delegates to it. updatePreferences() handles sms/webhook fields. Deep-copy helper covers all channels.
3. src/services/notification.service.ts — Line 162: Changed from email-only preference check to generic shouldSend() for all channels.
4. src/index.ts — Exported new types and constants.
5. tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts — Added 14 new tests for multi-channel scenarios (SMS/webhook enable/disable, per-channel categories, cross-channel independence, in_app fallthrough). Total 45 tests in this file.
